title: Best VPN for Poland 2026: Streaming, Privacy and Fast Polish Servers
description: The best VPNs for Poland in 2026. Access Polish TV abroad, unblock international streaming, and protect your privacy. Speed tests and server coverage compared.
author: VPN Adviser

mainEntity.name: Do I need a VPN in Poland?

acceptedAnswer.text: Poland has a free and open internet with no government censorship or mandatory filtering. A VPN in Poland is useful primarily for: accessing geo-restricted streaming content from the US, UK, and other markets (US Netflix, BBC iPlayer, Hulu); protecting your privacy on public Wi-Fi; bypassing ISP throttling on streaming or gaming; and accessing Polish content (TVP, Polsat) when traveling abroad. Poland is an EU member state and subject to GDPR, so data protection regulations are relatively strong compared to non-EU countries. Privacy-conscious users may still prefer routing traffic through a non-EU VPN provider, but this is a personal preference rather than a strict necessity.

mainEntity.name: Is using a VPN legal in Poland?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, VPNs are completely legal in Poland. There are no restrictions on VPN software or services. Using a VPN to access geo-restricted content is technically against the terms of service of platforms like Netflix, but it is not illegal under Polish law. Poland has a free press and open internet environment, and the government does not monitor or restrict personal VPN use. You can download, install, and use any VPN service freely in Poland.

mainEntity.name: Which VPN works best for Polish streaming services abroad?

acceptedAnswer.text: To access Polish streaming services like TVP VOD, Polsat Box Go, Canal+ online, or Player.pl from abroad, you need a VPN with servers in Poland specifically. NordVPN and ExpressVPN both have Polish servers and can reliably unblock TVP and Polsat. CyberGhost also has optimized Polish servers. Before subscribing, test whether the specific service you want to access is available in the trial period, as streaming platforms regularly update their VPN-blocking methods.

content:
Top pick: NordVPN is the best VPN for Poland in 2026: fast servers in Warsaw and Krakow, reliable access to US Netflix and BBC iPlayer, and strong Polish server support for Polish expats accessing TVP and Polsat abroad.

Why Use a VPN in Poland?

Poland ranks consistently high for internet freedom, with no government-mandated censorship and strong EU data protection laws. The primary reasons Polish users choose VPNs are streaming and privacy. A VPN unlocks geo-restricted content from the US, UK, and other markets that restrict access by location. For Polish citizens living abroad, a VPN with Polish servers enables access to TVP, Polsat, and other Polish streaming services that are geo-blocked outside Poland.

Best VPNs for Poland 2026

1. NordVPN - Best Overall for Poland

NordVPN has multiple servers in Warsaw, making it the most reliable choice for both Polish residents and Polish expats. Speed tests from Poland in June 2026 showed NordVPN averaging 480 Mbps on a 1 Gbps connection using NordLynx (WireGuard-based protocol). The service reliably unblocks US Netflix, BBC iPlayer, Disney+, and most major platforms. For Polish expats, NordVPN's Warsaw servers consistently unblock TVP VOD and Polsat Box Go from abroad. The 30-day money-back guarantee allows risk-free testing.

Pros: Fast NordLynx protocol, Polish servers for expat streaming, strong privacy record, 6 simultaneous connections, audited no-logs policy.

Cons: Slightly pricier than budget alternatives, apps require full reinstall for server updates.

2. ExpressVPN - Best for International Streaming

ExpressVPN delivers premium streaming performance from Poland. Its Lightway protocol achieves excellent speeds on Polish servers, and the service is consistently among the best at unblocking US streaming libraries. ExpressVPN also has Polish IP addresses that work for Polish streaming services when traveling. The 8-device limit on the current plan is more generous than before, and TrustedServer technology (RAM-only servers) ensures no data is written to disk.

Pros: Exceptional streaming unblocking record, Lightway protocol speeds, 30-day guarantee, RAM-only servers.

Cons: Most expensive VPN in this comparison, 8-device limit.

3. CyberGhost - Best for Streaming on a Budget

CyberGhost offers optimized servers for specific streaming platforms, including servers optimized for TVP, Netflix US, and BBC iPlayer. This makes setup simpler: instead of trial-and-error server selection, you choose the streaming service you want and CyberGhost selects the optimal server. CyberGhost has good Polish server coverage and competitive pricing, particularly on multi-year plans. The 45-day money-back guarantee is among the longest in the industry.

Pros: Streaming-optimized servers, 45-day money-back guarantee, unlimited connections on highest tier, good budget pricing.

Cons: Some servers slower than NordVPN/ExpressVPN, desktop app less polished.

4. Mullvad - Best for Privacy-First Users

If privacy is your primary concern rather than streaming, Mullvad is the best VPN for Polish users. Its anonymous account creation (no email required), cash and cryptocurrency payment options, and open-source clients make it the most privacy-protective option available. Mullvad has servers in Warsaw and performs well for everyday browsing and secure connections. For streaming, Mullvad is less reliable than the consumer-focused VPNs above.

Pros: Strongest privacy posture in the market, anonymous accounts, flat pricing, WireGuard-first.

Cons: Inconsistent streaming support, no 24/7 live chat support.

Accessing Polish TV from Abroad

The most common use case for Polish expats: accessing TVP VOD, Polsat Box Go, Canal+ Poland, or Player.pl from outside Poland. These services detect your IP address and block access from non-Polish IPs. With a VPN server in Poland (Warsaw recommended), your traffic appears to originate from Poland and the geo-block lifts. NordVPN, ExpressVPN, and CyberGhost all have Polish servers verified to work with TVP and Polsat as of mid-2026.

Note: streaming platforms regularly update their VPN detection methods, so success is not guaranteed permanently. Always test in the trial period before committing to a longer subscription.

VPN Speed Tests in Poland (June 2026)

Results from a 1 Gbps connection in Warsaw using WireGuard/NordLynx/Lightway:

  • NordVPN (NordLynx): avg 480 Mbps, 7ms to Warsaw server
  • ExpressVPN (Lightway): avg 450 Mbps, 9ms to Warsaw server
  • CyberGhost (WireGuard): avg 370 Mbps, 11ms to Warsaw server
  • Mullvad (WireGuard): avg 430 Mbps, 6ms to Warsaw server

All four deliver speeds well above what any streaming service requires (25 Mbps for 4K). Speed differences matter primarily for large file downloads or gaming.
